content.text: Here are the test results and a screenshot of the failing assertion:

content.content:
running 5 tests
test commands::filter::test_empty_query ... ok
test commands::filter::test_by_tag ... FAILED
test commands::filter::test_by_priority ... ok
test commands::filter::test_combined ... ok
test models::task::test_new ... ok

failures:

---- commands::filter::test_by_tag stdout ----
thread 'commands::filter::test_by_tag' panicked at 'assertion failed: result.contains("work")', src/commands/filter.rs:89:5

test result: FAILED. 4 passed; 1 failed

content.text: I can see the test failure and the screenshot. The `test_by_tag` test fails because the filter function does a case-sensitive tag comparison, but the test expects case-insensitive matching. The screenshot confirms the tag 'Work' (capitalized) doesn't match the filter 'work' (lowercase). Let me fix the comparison.
I also found this issue in the storage module:
It's not using pretty-printed JSON. The data file is one huge line and impossible to debug manually. Can you fix that too?

Good catch — using `to_string_pretty` instead of `to_string` makes the data file human-readable, which is really helpful for debugging. The performance difference is negligible for the amount of data TaskTracker handles.
After your fixes, here's the new test output:
All green! Can you commit these two fixes?
All 5 tests pass now. I'll commit both fixes — the case-insensitive tag filtering and the pretty-printed JSON storage.

input.command: cd /home/alex/projects/tasktracker && git add -A && git commit -m "Fix case-insensitive tag filter and use pretty JSON for storage"
